name: MCV_integration_hotspots
description: Merkel cell polyomavirus (MCV) integrates monoclonally into the genome of every MCV-positive Merkel-cell carcinoma (MCC), with viral LT consistently truncated upstream of the helicase/origin-binding domain but downstream of the RB-binding LxCxE motif — this 'helicase-dead, RB-binding-intact' configuration is the molecular hallmark of MCV-driven MCC. Integration sites are scattered across the human genome with no single recurrent host locus; concatemeric integrations (multiple tandem viral copies plus host fragments) are common. The viral breakpoint clusters around the C-terminus of LT (~nt 1500-2000).
expected_copy_count: 1-15 clonally-integrated tandem copies per MCC tumor
